steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Translate "$x$ is 33% of $y$" into an equation, then solve for $y$ in terms of $x$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
Writing the percentage statement as an equation gives $x=\frac{33}{100}y$. Multiply both sides by $\frac{100}{33}$ to isolate $y$: $y=\frac{100}{33}x$.
  • Choice A — $\frac{100}{33}x$: matches exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — $\frac{10}{33}x$: off by a factor of 10; comes from treating 33% as $\frac{3.3}{100}$ rather than $\frac{33}{100}$. ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{1}{33}x$: omits the factor of 100 entirely; this would give $y$ in terms of a 3300% relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — $\frac{67}{100}x$: uses $100-33=67$, confusing "33% of $y$" with "$y$ minus 33% of $y$". ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
